title: RGB-to-RGBA conversion (3-channel to 4-channel expansion) runs on the main thread inside drawCanvas, blocking the UI for large images.
description: The `drawCanvas` function runs a tight loop converting RGB to RGBA on the main thread: `for (let i = 0, j = 0; i < rgb.length; i += 3, j += 4)`. For a 1800px lightbox image (~9.7M pixels), this loop touches ~29 MB of source data with 9.7M iterations. This runs synchronously on the main thread every time a lightbox image is displayed or a live update fires. The same work is already done in WASM via `rgb_to_rgba` in the worker for the JXL encode — a parallel path should be used here. In the live path (line 409-414), the same loop runs on every slider move, causing jank.

title: Live lightbox update also performs the RGB-to-RGBA expansion on the main thread inline in the message handler.
description: The `liveHandler` callback executes `for (let i = 0, j = 0; i < msg.rgb.length; i += 3, j += 4)` directly in the message handler, which runs on the main thread. Since `msg.rgb` for the lightbox can be up to 1800px on the long edge (~9.7 MP), this expansion blocks the event loop for tens of milliseconds per slider change. The worker could send RGBA directly (at the cost of 33% more IPC data) or the worker could call `rgb_to_rgba` before posting the live message, keeping the main thread free.

title: thumbRgb and bigRgb are sent as transferable buffers, but fullRgb is retained in the worker for rgb_to_rgba — causing an extra full-res copy.
description: `fullRgb` (full resolution RGB8, potentially 60+ MB for a 20 MP sensor) is kept alive in the worker to be passed to `rgb_to_rgba` for JXL encoding. Meanwhile `thumbRgb.buffer` and `bigRgb.buffer` are transferred. The `downscale_rgb` call at line 196 copies `fullRgb` into WASM memory, returns a new JS Uint8Array, and then frees the WASM copy — but `fullRgb` itself remains valid in JS. After `bigRgb` is posted, `fullRgb` (~60MB) stays alive until `rgb_to_rgba(fullRgb)` at line 212. If JXL encoding is slow (seconds), this large buffer is held in the worker's heap for that entire duration, preventing GC. Passing the RGBA-converted version instead and freeing `fullRgb` earlier would reduce peak worker memory.

title: triggerGalleryLiveUpdate calls cards.filter() and c._taskId lookups on every debounced slider event.
description: `triggerGalleryLiveUpdate` iterates the full `cards` array to find selected cards with `_taskId` on every gallery live update. For large batches (hundreds of files), this is an O(n) scan per slider event firing at ~12 Hz (80ms debounce). The `cardByTaskId` Map already provides O(1) lookups by taskId, but the inverse direction (card → selected?) requires a scan. Maintaining a `selectedCards` Set that is updated on select/deselect toggles would reduce this to O(selected) instead of O(total).

title: card.querySelector() is called multiple times in the onThumb/onDone callbacks for each file, inside async closures.
description: The `onThumb` and `onDone` message handlers each call `card.querySelector('canvas')`, `card.querySelector('.thumb-dl-btn')`, `card.querySelector('.download')`, `card.querySelector('.size')`, `card.querySelector('.time')` separately. These are O(subtree) DOM traversals. For a batch of 100 files completing simultaneously, this is 500+ querySelector calls in a tight callback loop. Caching the element references at `makeCard` time (storing them on the card element or in a closure) would eliminate repeated traversals.

title: canvas.width and canvas.height are reassigned on every drawCanvas call, clearing the canvas and forcing a layout recalculation even when dimensions have not changed.
description: Setting `canvas.width = w` unconditionally clears the canvas and triggers a style recalculation in the browser, even if the dimensions are identical to the current ones. For the gallery thumb live updates (which fire repeatedly on slider changes), the thumb canvas dimensions are constant after the initial render. A guard `if (canvas.width !== w || canvas.height !== h)` before reassigning would avoid the unnecessary clear-and-restyle cycle in the steady state.

title: rgb_to_rgba result is wrapped in Uint8ClampedArray by copy — a zero-copy path using the buffer directly would save an allocation.
description: `rgb_to_rgba(fullRgb)` returns a `Uint8Array` (from WASM-generated `.slice()`). It is then wrapped with `new Uint8ClampedArray(rgba)`, which copies the underlying buffer. `Uint8ClampedArray` and `Uint8Array` share the same underlying memory representation; the copy can be avoided by using `new Uint8ClampedArray(rgba.buffer, rgba.byteOffset, rgba.byteLength)`. For a 20 MP image, this avoids a ~80 MB allocation and copy before JXL encoding.
code_excerpt:
const rgba = rgb_to_rgba(fullRgb);
const imageData = { data: new Uint8ClampedArray(rgba), width: w, height: h };
